title: Creamy Coconut Jamaican Vegetable Curry
description: A peppery and silky monsoon Jamaican dish featuring a hint of powdered sugar, this curry is a delightful blend of flavors, served in a traditional lotus-leaf-wrap vessel, and is Jain-friendly, making it accessible to a wide range of diets.

ingredients.name: mixed vegetables
ingredients.notes: such as bell peppers, carrots, and potatoes

instructions: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger, and saute until the onion is translucent.
Add the curry powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the mixed vegetables, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the powdered sugar and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Deglaze the pan with a little water if the curry is too thick.
Serve the curry in lotus-leaf-wraps, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
